summary: A reported orchestrator-plus-sub-agent coding workflow appears to have reduced main-thread context load in one rewrite task, but the success claim remains tightly bounded by thin verification.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The workflow used a main orchestrator dialogue that delegated tasks to sub-agents, then verified and integrated their outputs during a LiteLLM provider rewrite.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence supports the reported workflow structure and task scope in this case, but not any broader claim that this structure is generally superior.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The experiment completed in about four hours of agent time with about half an hour of human effort, 16 sub-agent sessions, and a total spend of $13.86.
bounded_claims.bounds: These are reported case metrics for one run; they do not show which parts of the workflow caused the efficiency or whether similar results would repeat elsewhere.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The app appears to have worked in this case, but verification coverage was limited.
bounded_claims.bounds: The source supports only a bounded functionality claim because observed operation and five green tests are too thin to establish broad correctness, robustness, or reliability.
